chunks.en: I usually pick up a pull request review right after my morning standup, when my head's still clear.
chunks.ja: 私はたいてい、頭がまだすっきりしている朝会の直後にプルリクのレビューに取りかかります。

chunks.en: The first thing I check in the diff is whether the tests actually cover the new logic.
chunks.ja: 差分を見るとき最初に確認するのは、テストが新しいロジックをちゃんとカバーしているかどうかです。

chunks.en: To avoid sounding harsh, I frame my comments as questions like "What do you think about renaming this?"
chunks.ja: きつく聞こえないように、「これをリネームするのはどう思う?」のように質問の形でコメントを書くようにしています。

chunks.en: I only approve it once the code is clean and there are no blocking issues left.
chunks.ja: コードがきれいで、ブロックするような問題が残っていないと確認できたときだけ、私はapproveします。

outline: Opening: when and how I start a review
Point 1: what I check first in the diff
Wrap-up: what makes me approve or request changes

modelTalk.text: Honestly, I usually pick up a review first thing in the morning, before I dive into my own coding. If I leave it too long, it just piles up and blocks my teammate. When I open the diff, I check the overall structure first, not the tiny details. I want to grasp what problem they're solving before I nitpick anything. Then I look at edge cases and whether the tests actually cover them. When I leave comments, I try to sound curious, not bossy. Instead of "this is wrong," I'll ask, "what do you think about handling it this way?" I always add a positive note too, because nobody likes only criticism. If it's clean and does the job, I'll approve it straight away. But if something might break in production, I'd ask for changes, even if it slows us down. At the end of the day, we're all on the same side.

chunks.en: I usually pick up a pull request first thing in the morning, before I dive into my own coding.
chunks.ja: 私はたいてい、自分のコーディングに取りかかる前の朝一番にプルリクエストに手をつけます。

chunks.en: The first thing I check in the diff is whether the change actually matches what the ticket asked for.
chunks.ja: 差分で最初に確認するのは、その変更が本当にチケットの要求どおりになっているかどうかです。

chunks.en: To avoid sounding harsh, I usually frame my comments as questions like "What do you think about extracting this?"
chunks.ja: きつく聞こえないように、私はたいていコメントを「これを切り出すのはどう思う？」のような質問の形にしています。

chunks.en: What makes me approve it is clean, tested code, but I'll ask for changes if the logic feels risky.
chunks.ja: approveする決め手はきれいでテスト済みのコードですが、ロジックが危うそうなら修正をお願いします。

outline: Opening: when and how I start a review
Point 1: what I check first in the diff
Wrap-up: my approve vs. request-changes criteria

modelTalk.text:
To be honest, I usually don't dive into a review the second it lands. I'll finish whatever I'm on, then pick it up when my head's clear. Rushing it never does anyone any favours.

When I open the diff, I check the overall shape first — is the change actually solving the right problem? After that, I zoom into the tricky bits, like edge cases and error handling.

Leaving comments is where I really watch my tone. Instead of "this is wrong", I'll ask something like, "What do you reckon about handling it this way?" It softens things and keeps it collaborative.

As for approving — if it works, it's readable, and the tests pass, I'll happily sign off. Small nitpicks? I'll flag them but won't block the merge.

I only ask for changes when something could genuinely break, or the logic's hard to follow. That said, I always try to explain why, not just say no.
